title: Canonical Benefits Person ID
description: The system shall maintain a canonical Benefits Person ID (benefitsPersonId) for each employee and dependent as the primary enterprise identifier for reconciliation. This ID is internal and does not equal the HR employee ID, carrier member ID, or payroll employee ID.

title: Cross-Reference Identity Mappings
description: The system shall maintain persistent cross-reference mappings between Benefits Person ID, HR employee ID, carrier member IDs (one per carrier/product), and payroll employee ID. These mappings shall be effective-dated to support carrier transitions.

title: Cross-System IDs Must Not Be Assumed Equal
description: The system shall not assume that employee IDs are identical across HR, carrier, and payroll systems. The reconciliation engine shall always resolve identities through the cross-reference mapping, never by direct ID comparison across systems.

title: Multiple Carrier Member IDs Per Employee
description: The system shall support multiple carrier member IDs for a single employee — one per benefit product or carrier. An employee enrolled in Medical (Aetna), Dental (Delta Dental), and Vision (VSP) shall have three separate carrier member ID mappings.

title: Dependent Identity Reconciliation
description: The system shall support separate identity reconciliation for dependents. Each dependent shall have a Benefits Dependent ID (benefitsDependentId) and carrier-specific member ID mappings. Reconciliation shall be performed at both employee and dependent level.

title: Composite Reconciliation Key
description: The system shall use a composite reconciliation key for three-way matching: benefitsPersonId + planId + planVersionId + coverageTier + coverageEffectiveDate. This prevents false-positive matches when an employee has multiple plans, coverage changes, or mid-year transitions.

title: Effective-Dated Identity Mapping Resolution
description: The system shall resolve external identifiers (carrier member ID, payroll ID) using effective-dated mappings that reflect carrier transitions. A reconciliation record shall always use the mapping that was active on the coverage effective date, not the current mapping.

title: Field-Level Three-Way Comparison
description: The system shall compare benefits, carrier, and payroll records at both identity and field levels. Field comparison shall include: plan, plan version, coverage tier, coverage effective date, coverage termination date, employee premium, employer contribution, and payroll deduction.

title: Identity Mapping Missing — Reconciliation Exception
description: The system shall identify missing identity mappings as reconciliation exceptions with status IDENTITY_MAPPING_MISSING. The UI shall display the affected employee, missing mapping type, and resolution actions (create mapping, search carrier record, resolve duplicate).

title: Duplicate and Conflicting Identity Mapping Detection
description: The system shall detect duplicate or conflicting identity mappings (e.g., two Benefits Person IDs mapped to the same carrier member ID, or two carrier member IDs mapped to the same employee for the same plan). These shall be raised as reconciliation exceptions.

title: Source Identifiers Retained for Audit
description: The system shall retain the original source identifiers from each external system (carrier file, payroll export) on every reconciliation record. These shall not be overwritten by the canonical identity resolution process.

title: Reconciliation Audit Trail
description: The system shall maintain an audit trail for each reconciliation run showing: composite key used, identity mappings resolved, fields compared, comparison results, reconciliation status, exceptions raised, and resolution actions taken.

demoSeed.ACM_E001: MATCHED — all identities and fields reconcile
demoSeed.ACM_E003: PLAN_MISMATCH — carrier has PPO-1000, benefits has PPO-500
